name: TodaySnapshot
description: The day's activity state the Today dashboard renders, sourced from HealthKit. healthKitConnected drives the day-one 'Connect Apple Health' empty state; when false the dashboard shows the friendly connect hero instead of metrics. Production starts empty (no snapshot, treated as not connected). Each scenario seeds one snapshot via flat rb-prefixed UserDefaults keys in deviceState.preferences (e.g. rbConnected, rbSteps, rbGoalSteps, rbActiveMinutes, rbDistanceMiles, rbMinutesSinceMovement, rbDate) read at launch.

fields.name: date
fields.description: ISO date for the snapshot, e.g. 2026-06-22

fields.name: healthKitConnected
fields.description: Whether Apple Health is connected; false => day-one connect hero

fields.name: minutesSinceLastMovement
fields.description: Minutes since the last meaningful movement; drives inactivity nudges

name: Workout
description: A recent run/walk/ride from HealthKit or Strava. The most recent one renders as the 'Latest run/walk' card on the Today dashboard; the full set feeds weekly-load context. Empty list => no latest-activity card.

name: WeeklyLoad
description: Rolled-up weekly training-load snapshot shown on the Today dashboard and used as coach context. Drives injury-aware coaching (e.g. a spiking loadTrend triggers a caution recommendation).

name: CoachRecommendation
description: The AI running coach's output for the Today card, plus the Buddy mascot mood it drives. In MVP this is a mocked coach response (mock-coach mode), so it is modeled as a mock-api source rather than a persisted store. Conservative and injury-aware: safetyFlag marks a caution/rest recommendation.

fields.name: buddyMood
fields.description: resting | ready | jogging | cheering | concerned | celebrating | recovery

fields.name: headline
fields.description: Short coach headline, e.g. 'Easy movement day'

fields.name: recommendationType
fields.description: move | walk | run | rest | celebrate | caution

fields.name: safetyFlag
fields.description: True when an injury-aware caution applies (e.g. spiking load, reported pain)

name: AccountPrefs
description: Optional account-backed copy of a signed-in user's settings/preferences (e.g. step goal), synced to the Supabase backend keyed to the stable Apple userID so it carries across reinstalls/devices. Written only when the user enables the 'Sync my settings' opt-in; guests never have a row. Mirrors local Preferences (UserDefaults) via last-write-wins on updated_at. Reuses the Strava PostgREST + service-role pattern. Contains NO health data by design (the prefs endpoint rejects health fields).

fields.name: user_id
fields.description: Stable Apple Sign in with Apple userID; primary key

fields.name: prefs
fields.description: JSON blob of synced settings/preferences (e.g. goalSteps, reminder prefs)

name: AccountHealth
description: Optional account-backed snapshot of a signed-in user's health/activity data, synced to the Supabase backend keyed to the Apple userID. OFF BY DEFAULT — written only after the user passes the explicit consent step and enables the separate 'Sync my health & activity data' opt-in. Stored in a distinct table from AccountPrefs so revoking/deleting health sync never touches preferences; turning health sync off deletes this row. Last-write-wins on updated_at. Never sent to analytics.

fields.name: health
fields.description: JSON blob of the derived health/activity snapshot uploaded only on opt-in

name: CoachProfile
description: Lightweight personalization profile collected during the onboarding flow and stored on-device (UserDefaults-backed, single JSON key), following the RaceStore pattern. All fields optional so a skipped onboarding step is representable as nil. Attached as an optional field on TodayState and threaded into coach context; it leaves the device only on connected-coach requests (never sent to analytics). The daily step goal is intentionally NOT duplicated here — it stays the single source of truth in UserPreferences.

fields.name: walkVolume
fields.description: How much the user usually walks: rarely | someDays | mostDays | daily; nil => skipped

fields.name: walkTime
fields.description: When the user usually walks: mornings | midday | evenings | varies; nil => skipped

fields.name: otherTraining
fields.description: Array of other training the user does: running | strength | cycling | mobility | sports; empty/absent => none

name: RaceGoal
description: An optional upcoming race the user tells Otterpace about, stored entirely on-device (UserDefaults-backed JSON array under the rbRacesJSON/otterpaceRaces key via RaceStore — same pattern as workouts). Carried as a list on TodayState (default empty) so it flows with no new plumbing into all three coaching engines: the on-device CoachEngine, the remote AI coach (api/coach.ts), and the WeeklyReviewEngine. Coaching keys off the soonest race whose date is today-or-later and the whole-days-until it, branching build (>21d) / approaching / taper (<=7d) / race-day (0d) — always subordinate to the hard safety rules. Never sent to analytics; most users have none.

fields.name: distanceMiles
fields.description: Race distance in miles; presets 5K/10K/Half/Marathon or a custom value

fields.name: date
fields.description: ISO yyyy-MM-dd race date, matching workout date format; ISO strings sort lexicographically for 'soonest upcoming'

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Optional site details: start area, corral, goal time
